terrain: Rigid flat surface, friction μ randomised per trial
terrain_rationale: Bekker-Wong at 15-28 cm sinkage produces forces 10-20x Curiosity weight (899 kg); rigid low-μ terrain creates the same observable entrapment signal (high wheel slip, near-zero progress) without initialization artifacts.
fidelity_gap_notes: Rigid body solver: Chrono NSC (complementarity) vs training Newton (MPM implicit) — different contact resolution method.
Terrain: rigid surface with variable friction vs training MPM continuum elasto-plasticity — different physics class, not a parameter perturbation.
Rover mass: Curiosity 899 kg vs AAU ~35 kg; wheel radius 0.25 m vs 0.10 m. Genuine morphological domain gap.
Per-wheel drive commands issued individually via per-wheel SpeedDriver motor function SetSetpoint; steer via per-wheel SteerMotorFunc.
